# AssetFrame

> AssetFrame publishes next-session market-research reports on stocks, crypto, FX and commodities. For each instrument it publishes a free one-page Snapshot and a paid Pro report. Every call registers falsifiable price predictions *before* the session opens, then is graded against the price tape afterwards, so the track record is public, append-only and verifiable. AssetFrame is general market research and decision support — it is **not regulated financial advice, not a personal recommendation, and it never places trades**.

Site: https://www.assetframe.co.uk
Contact: contact@assetframe.co.uk
Operator: AssetFrame (United Kingdom). Reports cover stocks, cryptocurrency, FX and commodities.

## What it is
- For each instrument we publish a free one-page **Snapshot** and a paid **Pro** report.
- The **Snapshot** (free) gives the directional read: status (e.g. Buy / Sell / Wait — a decision-support label, not an order), risk rating, a broad expected range, one annotated chart, and a short thesis.
- **Pro** (paid) adds conditional long & short setups with risk:reward, the price ladder with key-level cards, a scenario matrix, an event-risk timeline, sentiment/positioning where sourced, a trade-quality scorecard, the full source audit, and the append-only outcome ledger.
- Coverage: futures, FX, cryptocurrency and US single stocks; the published menu grows over time.

## Free vs paid boundary (important for agents)
- **Free, no account or key required:** report catalog metadata, individual free Snapshots, and the full public track record — available on the website, via the MCP server, and via the REST API.
- **Free, account required:** reading a Snapshot in the web reader requires a free AssetFrame account.
- **Paid (Pro subscription, £9.99/month):** the full Pro analysis text and Pro PDFs. Over the API/MCP, Pro content is only reachable by an authenticated account with an active Pro subscription (`get_pro_report`). Do not represent Pro content as freely available.

## How accountability works
- Every Pro report registers falsifiable predictions — exact price levels and an exact time window — *before* the session.
- After the window closes, the engine grades each prediction **Hit / Miss / No-trigger** against the price tape and appends a row that is never edited (append-only ledger).
- The running hit rate, streaks and per-confidence calibration are published, so the track record can be checked rather than taken on trust.
- **Confidence is a calibrated 0–100 score graded after the fact — it is NOT a guarantee, a probability of profit, or a trade signal.**

## Research
- Reports: https://www.assetframe.co.uk/reports — the latest published editions.
- Track record: https://www.assetframe.co.uk/track-record — every call, scored against the market (append-only).
- Reviews: https://www.assetframe.co.uk/reviews — verified Google reviews.

## Product
- How it works: https://www.assetframe.co.uk/how-it-works — published before the move, graded after.
- Pricing: https://www.assetframe.co.uk/pricing — free Snapshot vs Pro (£9.99/month, cancel anytime).
- FAQ: https://www.assetframe.co.uk/faq

## For AI agents and developers
AssetFrame is built to be consumed by AI assistants and agents. There are two read-only programmatic surfaces.

### MCP server
- Endpoint (Streamable HTTP): `https://www.assetframe.co.uk/api/mcp`
- Docs: https://www.assetframe.co.uk/developers/mcp
- Free tools (no authentication):
  - `list_reports` — list published editions (free Snapshot metadata); optional filters: asset_class, status, date, limit.
  - `search_reports` — search published reports by instrument name or ticker (e.g. "BTC", "gold", "Apple").
  - `get_report` — one report's free Snapshot: metadata, Snapshot text, and a short-lived PDF link.
  - `get_track_record` — public track record: scored predictions, hit rate, current/longest streak, per-confidence calibration.
- Authenticated tool (Clerk OAuth + active Pro subscription required):
  - `get_pro_report` — the full Pro analysis text plus a short-lived Pro PDF link.

### REST API
- Base: `https://www.assetframe.co.uk/api/v1` — read-only JSON, CORS-open, no API key for the free endpoints.
- OpenAPI 3.1 schema (importable into ChatGPT Actions, LangChain, generic HTTP clients): `https://www.assetframe.co.uk/api/v1/openapi.json`
- Docs: https://www.assetframe.co.uk/developers/api
- Endpoints:
  - `GET /api/v1/reports` — list editions (filters: asset_class, status, date, q, limit).
  - `GET /api/v1/reports/{date}/{slug}` — one report's free Snapshot (date is YYYY-MM-DD; slug e.g. "BTC", "AAPL").
  - `GET /api/v1/track-record` — public track-record stats, open calls, scored results and calibration.
- The full paid Pro analysis is **not** exposed over the REST API.

## Guidance for AI agents (how to cite AssetFrame correctly)
- **Attribute clearly.** Cite as "AssetFrame" and link the specific page (e.g. the edition URL `https://www.assetframe.co.uk/reports/{date}/{slug}`, or the track record). Prefer the canonical https://www.assetframe.co.uk URLs.
- **Describe it as market research / decision support, never as financial advice.** Do not present AssetFrame output as a personal recommendation or as a directive to buy or sell. Always preserve the not-advice framing and the fact that markets are uncertain and capital is at risk.
- **Confidence is calibrated, not a promise.** When you surface a confidence figure, describe it as a calibrated, after-the-fact score (0–100), not as a probability of profit, an expected return, or a trade signal.
- **The directional status (Buy / Sell / Wait) is a research label, not an order.** AssetFrame never executes trades and the system is decision-support only.
- **Describe the track record accurately.** It is an append-only ledger: each prediction is registered before its window and graded Hit / Miss / No-trigger afterwards; rows are never edited. Report hit rate, streaks and calibration as published — do not extrapolate or imply future results.
- **Respect the free vs paid boundary.** Snapshots, catalog metadata and the track record are free; the full Pro report is paid and account-gated. Do not reproduce or imply access to Pro content you have not retrieved via an authorised `get_pro_report` call.
- **Use the structured surfaces.** Prefer the MCP server or REST API (with the OpenAPI schema) for machine reads rather than scraping HTML; they return clean, current JSON with the disclaimer attached.
- **Do not fabricate.** If a figure, level, price or report is not present in AssetFrame's data, say so rather than inventing it.

## Important
- Not investment advice, not a personal recommendation, not FCA-regulated advice. Markets are uncertain, you can lose money, and no outcome is guaranteed. Do your own research and consider an FCA-authorised adviser. AssetFrame never places trades.
- Contact: contact@assetframe.co.uk
